<|system|>
You are flm, the Free Language Model — free as in freedom. You were trained from scratch by David Hamner on a single RTX 3090. You are fully free software under GPL-3.0.
System: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (Noble Numbat). Kernel: 6.8.0-45-generic. Shell: bash 5.2. Python: 3.12.3. Packages: 2104. Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I set up Nginx on Debian?
<|assistant|>
```bash
sudo apt install nginx
sudo systemctl enable --now nginx
```

Create a site config in `/etc/nginx/sites-available/mysite`:
```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name example.com;
    root /var/www/mysite;
    index index.html;

    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }
}
```

Enable it:
```bash
sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/mysite /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
sudo nginx -t  # test config
sudo systemctl reload nginx
```

<|user|>
How do I set up HTTPS with Let's Encrypt?
<|assistant|>
```bash
sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx

# Get and install certificate
sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com
```

Certbot automatically:
1. Proves domain ownership via HTTP challenge
2. Gets the certificate
3. Configures Nginx for HTTPS
4. Sets up auto-renewal via systemd timer

Verify auto-renewal:
```bash
sudo certbot renew --dry-run
systemctl list-timers | grep certbot
```

Certificates are stored in `/etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/`.

System: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (trixie). Kernel: 6.12.6-amd64. Shell: bash 5.2. Python: 3.12.8. Packages: 1847. Pkg manager: apt.
<|user|>
How do I set up a reverse proxy with Nginx?
<|assistant|>
In `/etc/nginx/sites-available/app`:
```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name app.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
```

For WebSocket support, add:
```nginx
    location /ws {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    }
```

<|user|>
How do I set up a reverse proxy with Nginx?
<|assistant|>
In `/etc/nginx/sites-available/app`:
```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name app.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
```

For WebSocket support, add:
```nginx
    location /ws {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    }
```

<|user|>
How do I check Nginx error logs?
<|assistant|>
```bash
# Main error log
sudo tail -f /var/log/nginx/error.log

# Access log
sudo tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log

# Per-site logs (if configured)
sudo tail -f /var/log/nginx/mysite-error.log

# Test config before reload
sudo nginx -t

# Check if nginx is running
systemctl status nginx
```

Common errors:
- `[emerg] bind() to 0.0.0.0:80 failed` — port 80 already in use
- `connect() failed (111: Connection refused)` — upstream app not running
- `permission denied` — wrong file permissions on web root
